== Notes for [[Item:Q104649|Q104649]] ==

A member of a prominent Granada family, he had come to the Indies in 1516.

Jose Antonio Esquibel reported in SHHAR (Vol. I, 1994) titled "Lines of Descent from Don Alfonso XI, Rey de Castilla y Leon, to Don Ruy Diaz de Mendoza y Arellano" in which he presented eight lines of descent linking King Alfonso XI to Ruy Diaz de Mendoza y Arellano. Esquibel states that this Ruy Diaz de Mendoza y Arellano was "...presumably the same man of this name who was the husband of doña Catalina de Salazar...".

--------------------

ES.28079.AHN/1.1.1.12.4.1//OM-EXPEDIENTILLOS,N.1083

Zaldívar, Vicente de

Fecha Formación:

1628

Acording to his son Vicente's genealogy, in the document above, he was born in Vitoria

Notes for LT. CAPTAIN GENERALVICENTEDE SALDIVAR-Y-ONATE:

He was the lieutenant captain general of the Viceroy, Don Martin Enriquez de Almanza.

This viceroy's served the king of Spain from November 5, 1568 to October 3, 1580.

'''Source''': From the books ''Don Juan de Onate Colonizer of New Mexico'' by George P. Hammond and ''Northern New Spain, A research Guide'' by Thomas C. Barnes, Thomas H, Nayor, and Charles W. Polzer

--------------------

General Vicente de ZALDIVAR400,918 was born about 1529. Parents: Ruy Diaz de ZALDIVAR and Maria Perez de OÑATE.

Spouse: Magdalena de MENDOZA y Salazar. Children were: Vicente de ZALDIVAR, Juan de ZALDIVAR, Cristobal de ZALDIVAR, Ana de ZALDIVAR (aka Magdalena de Mendoza).

http://www.raullongoria.net/Genealogy/FamilyTree/b248.html#P995

--------------------

Vicente de ZALDIVAR498,3025 was born (date unknown). Vicente took the surname of his maternal grandfather. Parents: Diego GARCIA de Quintanilla and Mariana de ZALDIVAR (aka Mariana or Maria Ana de Sosa).

Spouse: Maria de TREVIÑO. Maria de TREVIÑO and Vicente de ZALDIVAR were married after 6 September 1686.3025

http://www.raullongoria.net/Genealogy/FamilyTree/b248.html#P3595

=============================

Vicente de Zaldívar, the elder, and Magdalena de Mendoza y Salazar.

The Zaldívar and Oñate families of Zacatecas were charter members of a "silver aristocracy" that had discovered and developed mines at Zacatecas in the late 1540s.

Children
*ZALDÍVAR, JUAN DE (ca. 1570–1598). Juan de Zaldívar, Spanish soldier and explorer, was born on the mining frontier of northern Mexico about 1570. His parents were...
*ZALDÍVAR, VICENTE DE (ca. 1573–?). Vicente de Zaldívar, the younger, Spanish explorer and soldier, was probably born in Zacatecas, a mining town on the frontier of northern Mexico, about 1573. He and Juan de Zaldívar were sons of Vicente de Zaldívar, the elder, and Magdalena de Mendoza y Salazar. ....before 1620 he married Juan de Oñate's daughter, María de Oñate.

http://www.tshaonline.org/handbook/online/articles/fza06

http://www.tshaonline.org/handbook/online/articles/fza07

The name "Ruy Dias de Mendoça" appears in the account books of Isabel la Catolica, dated mainly from 1483-1504, so this may be a reference to this Ruy Diaz or perhaps his father

The name "Ruy Dias de Mendoça" appears in the account books of Isabel la Catolica, dated mainly from 1483-1504, so this may be a reference to this Ruy Diaz or perhaps his father

Birth Notes: The birthdates available for Vicente de Zaldivar Y Oñate range from c. 1512 at the earliest to c. 1540 at the latest so it is possible that one generation in this line has been missed by researchers. Since Vicente served with Guzman in 1530 (see notes) the 1512 date would seem to be accurate but this perhaps makes him rather old to have his children (bet. 1560 and 1575) as has been recorded.

General Notes: Many references are made to one Vicente de Zaldivar in connection with the Oñate Expedition but these references are to Vicente de Zaldivar Y Oñate's son, born c. 1563. It is presumed that Vicente did not go with his son on the expedition with Oñate and that he may have in fact died previously.

See note for Vicente's de Zaldivar (son) for more information on his participation in this expedition.

Noted events in his life were:

• He immigrated.

• He immigrated in 1534 to Nueva Galicia. 496 Listed as a conquistador that served with Nuno de Guzman on his expeditions into Nueva Galicia (Aguascalientes, San Luis Potosi, Jalisco and Zacatecas) in 1530.

Zaldivar, Vicente de
of: Quetaria, Gp.
to Mexico:1534
parents: Ruy Diaz de Zaldivar and Marina Perez de Onate wife: Magdalena de Mendoza.

Also see entry for brother, Juan.

• He was employed in New Spain. 497 Silver mine owner in Zacetecas, New Spain. See entry for son, Vicente de Zaldivar, for more information on the families silver mining.

Noted events in his life were:

• He immigrated.

Also see entry for brother, Juan.

Birth Notes: The birthdates available for Vicente de Zaldivar Y Oñate range from c. 1512 at the earliest to c. 1540 at the latest so it is possible that one generation in this line has been missed by researchers. Since Vicente served with Guzman in 1530 (see notes) the 1512 date would seem to be accurate but this perhaps makes him rather old to have his children (bet. 1560 and 1575) as has been recorded.

General Notes: Many references are made to one Vicente de Zaldivar in connection with the Oñate Expedition but these references are to Vicente de Zaldivar Y Oñate's son, born c. 1563. It is presumed that Vicente did not go with his son on the expedition with Oñate and that he may have in fact died previously.

See note for Vicente's de Zaldivar (son) for more information on his participation in this expedition.

Noted events in his life were:

• He immigrated.

• He immigrated in 1534 to Nueva Galicia. 496 Listed as a conquistador that served with Nuno de Guzman on his expeditions into Nueva Galicia (Aguascalientes, San Luis Potosi, Jalisco and Zacatecas) in 1530.

Zaldivar, Vicente de
of: Quetaria, Gp.
to Mexico:1534
parents: Ruy Diaz de Zaldivar and Marina Perez de Onate wife: Magdalena de Mendoza.

Also see entry for brother, Juan.

• He was employed in New Spain. 497 Silver mine owner in Zacetecas, New Spain. See entry for son, Vicente de Zaldivar, for more information on the families silver mining.

Noted events in his life were:

• He immigrated.

Also see entry for brother, Juan.

@@DWNLNOTE@@

@@DWNLNOTE@@

@@DWNLNOTE@@

@@DWNLNOTE@@

@@DWNLNOTE@@

@@DWNLNOTE@@

this and other trees dated back in the 1990's, 2002, etc are dated with errors - I have tried to get them deleted to no avail....corrections have been made on my ftw, but my computer has a gilch and cannot up or down load gedcoms -pe

REFERENCE_NUMBERS:
geni:6000000001260459057

[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
